episode_name: October 7 attack and southern Israel border-retake phase
geographic_scope: Attacked communities and civilian sites in southern Israel near Gaza, plus immediate cross-border combat corridor
theater_description: Short-window cross-border attack, hostage taking, civilian massacres, and immediate Israeli border-retake operations.
geometry_method_note: Bounded to attacked southern-Israel communities, nearby civilian sites, and the immediate cross-border combat corridor rather than all of Israel.
military_area_note: Covers Israeli and Hamas-led forces actually engaged in the attacked southern theater during the attack and immediate retake window.
civilian_area_note: Excludes the whole Israeli population and uses only the directly attacked southern footprint.
split_reason: Separated from later Gaza campaign phases because the geography, victims, and exposure logic are materially different.
quality_note: Deaths are better observed than exposure denominators; person-month rates are not defensible enough, so fallback is used.

episode_name: Gaza campaign phase 1: initial air campaign and high-intensity ground entry
theater_description: Initial strip-wide bombardment, siege conditions, and high-intensity ground entry with rapidly rising displacement.
geometry_method_note: Uses the Gaza Strip rather than Israel or the State of Palestine as a whole because civilian and combatant exposure was strip-specific.
military_area_note: Includes Israeli forces committed to the Gaza theater and Palestinian armed groups operating inside Gaza during the episode window.
civilian_area_note: Uses strip-wide civilian presence because violence and displacement affected the full strip during the episode window.
split_reason: Distinct from the October 7 attack phase and from the later lower-intensity Gaza phase after January 2024.
quality_note: Cumulative totals are strong, but combatant/civilian separation requires explicit modeling.

episode_name: Gaza campaign phase 2: lower-intensity but persistent strip-wide operations, south focus and raids
theater_description: Post-100-day phase with continuing operations, southern focus, recurrent raids, and mass displacement still affecting most of the strip.
geometry_method_note: Uses the Gaza Strip rather than Israel or the State of Palestine as a whole because civilian and combatant exposure was strip-specific.
military_area_note: Includes Israeli forces committed to the Gaza theater and Palestinian armed groups operating inside Gaza during the episode window.
civilian_area_note: Uses strip-wide civilian presence because violence and displacement affected the full strip during the episode window.
split_reason: Separated because the campaign shifted into a longer lower-intensity but still strip-wide raid and bombardment phase.
quality_note: Episode boundary is defensible, but civilian/combatant attribution is still weak and identified-fatality composition lags the headline total.

episode_name: Ceasefire I and residual violence / body-recovery period
theater_description: Formal ceasefire with continuing incidents, body retrieval from rubble, and residual lethal violence.
geometry_method_note: Uses the Gaza Strip rather than Israel or the State of Palestine as a whole because civilian and combatant exposure was strip-specific.
military_area_note: Includes Israeli forces committed to the Gaza theater and Palestinian armed groups operating inside Gaza during the episode window.
civilian_area_note: Uses strip-wide civilian presence because violence and displacement affected the full strip during the episode window.
split_reason: Separated because formal ceasefire conditions changed the violence profile and made the period analytically distinct.
quality_note: Core direct-death estimates were nulled after the mortality audit because the ceasefire-period reporting windows mix newly killed people with bodies newly retrieved or with casualties reported after attacks that mostly occurred before the ceasefire.

episode_name: Renewed offensive after ceasefire breakdown, pre-'Gideon's Chariots'
theater_description: Large-scale renewed strikes and operations after the January ceasefire collapsed.
geometry_method_note: Uses the Gaza Strip rather than Israel or the State of Palestine as a whole because civilian and combatant exposure was strip-specific.
military_area_note: Includes Israeli forces committed to the Gaza theater and Palestinian armed groups operating inside Gaza during the episode window.
civilian_area_note: Uses strip-wide civilian presence because violence and displacement affected the full strip during the episode window.
split_reason: Separated because the ceasefire breakdown reopened large-scale offensive activity before the later named operation phase.
quality_note: A rare first-month age/sex breakdown exists, but later weeks still need modeled allocation.

theater_description: Expanded bombardment and ground operations under the new operation name 'Gideon's Chariots'.
geometry_method_note: Uses the Gaza Strip rather than Israel or the State of Palestine as a whole because civilian and combatant exposure was strip-specific.
military_area_note: Includes Israeli forces committed to the Gaza theater and Palestinian armed groups operating inside Gaza during the episode window.
civilian_area_note: Uses strip-wide civilian presence because violence and displacement affected the full strip during the episode window.
split_reason: Separated because the later named operation materially changed the campaign framing and timing.
quality_note: Combatant deaths after January 2025 remain weakly sourced. The October 2025 endpoint also contains retroactively approved fatalities, so conflict-level direct-death use is stronger than precise within-phase timing assignment.

episode_name: Ceasefire II and post-ceasefire residual violence (open episode; estimates right-censored)
theater_description: Second ceasefire period with continued incidents, Israeli territorial deployment, and ongoing Palestinian deaths.
geometry_method_note: Uses the Gaza Strip rather than Israel or the State of Palestine as a whole because civilian and combatant exposure was strip-specific.
military_area_note: Includes Israeli forces committed to the Gaza theater and Palestinian armed groups operating inside Gaza during the episode window.
civilian_area_note: Uses strip-wide civilian presence because violence and displacement affected the full strip during the episode window.
split_reason: Separated because the second ceasefire period is an open, right-censored residual-violence phase rather than a full offensive window.
quality_note: Since-ceasefire killed counts are usable through 2026-02-25 when taken from explicit 'since the ceasefire agreement' series, but the episode remains open through the present date and civilian-status coding still relies on an OHCHR subset.
